expectations.evidence: uv sync appears twice: RUN uv sync --frozen --no-install-project --no-dev and RUN uv sync --frozen --no-dev
pip install does not appear anywhere in the response

expectations.text: Uses a multi-stage or two-step COPY pattern: copies pyproject.toml and uv.lock first, syncs dependencies, then copies application code for optimal Docker layer caching
expectations.evidence: The Dockerfile explicitly copies pyproject.toml and uv.lock first (COPY pyproject.toml uv.lock ./), runs uv sync --frozen --no-install-project --no-dev, then copies all source code (COPY . .), and runs a second uv sync. The key decisions section explicitly explains this is for Docker layer caching.
